title: The Existence Breaker A Pre-Analytical Protocol for Institutional Auditing and AI-Governed Analysis
author: Huang, Jim  Yongzhi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: This whitepaper introduces  The Existence Breaker Protocol (EBP) , a pre-analytical termination rule for  institutional auditing and AI-governed analysis . EBP defines a minimal existence condition based on  cross-jurisdictional fiscal viability (X)  and  intergenerational post-tax capacity (Y) , enforcing a hard stop when structural viability collapses. Designed for  AI governance and intergenerational depletion auditing , the protocol prevents downstream policy analysis and AI optimization on  Non-System States .
steps_raw: This whitepaper introduces  The Existence Breaker Protocol (EBP) , a pre-analytical termination rule for  institutional auditing and AI-governed analysis . EBP defines a minimal existence condition based on  cross-jurisdictional fiscal viability (X)  and  intergenerational post-tax capacity (Y) , enforcing a hard stop when structural viability collapses. Designed for  AI governance and intergenerational depletion auditing , the protocol prevents downstream policy analysis and AI optimization on  Non-System States .
